text:
## Compliance & Regulatory  
### Regulatory Requirements  
**FCA COBS:**
- Customer communication and fair treatment
- System reliability and business continuity
- Data security and privacy  
**PRA Requirements:**
- Capital adequacy monitoring
- Operational resilience
- Cyber security (Operational Resilience)  
**GDPR:**
- Data subject rights (access, deletion)
- Data protection by design
- Breach notification (72-hour requirement)
name: Chunk 33/39

raw_entities.name: Financial Conduct Authority
raw_entities.description: UK financial regulatory authority responsible for enforcing standards around customer communication, fair treatment, system reliability, business continuity, and data security within the banking sector.

raw_entities.name: Prudential Regulation Authority
raw_entities.description: UK regulatory body overseeing prudential standards for banks, specifically mandating capital adequacy monitoring, operational resilience frameworks, and cyber security controls to ensure institutional stability.

raw_entities.name: General Data Protection Regulation
raw_entities.description: Comprehensive data protection framework imposing strict obligations on customer data rights, privacy-by-design architecture, and mandatory breach reporting procedures for banking operations.

raw_entities.name: Customer Communication and Fair Treatment Requirement
raw_entities.description: Regulatory mandate requiring all banking communications, marketing materials, and customer interactions to adhere to fairness standards, transparency, and ethical treatment protocols.

raw_entities.name: System Reliability and Business Continuity Requirement
raw_entities.description: Operational constraint requiring banking technology infrastructure to maintain high availability, robust failover mechanisms, and uninterrupted service delivery during normal and stress conditions.

raw_entities.name: Data Security and Privacy Requirement
raw_entities.description: Regulatory obligation mandating the implementation of technical and procedural safeguards to protect sensitive customer and operational data from unauthorized access, leaks, or privacy violations.

raw_entities.name: Capital Adequacy Monitoring Requirement
raw_entities.description: PRA-enforced control requiring continuous tracking, reporting, and stress-testing of financial reserves to ensure the bank maintains sufficient capital buffers against potential losses.

raw_entities.name: Operational Resilience Requirement
raw_entities.description: Regulatory standard requiring the bank to maintain critical services, establish impact tolerances, and recover quickly from operational disruptions or technology failures.

raw_entities.name: Cyber Security Requirement
raw_entities.description: PRA-mandated control requiring robust technical defenses, threat monitoring, and incident response protocols to protect banking infrastructure and data from cyber threats.

raw_entities.name: Data Subject Rights Requirement
raw_entities.description: GDPR obligation ensuring customers retain enforceable rights to access their personal data and request its deletion upon valid legal grounds.

raw_entities.name: Data Protection by Design Requirement
raw_entities.description: GDPR principle requiring privacy safeguards, data minimization techniques, and security controls to be integrated into the architecture of systems and processes from inception.

raw_entities.name: Breach Notification Requirement
raw_entities.description: Mandatory procedural rule requiring the bank to formally report confirmed data security incidents to relevant regulators and impacted individuals within a strict timeframe.

raw_entities.name: 72-Hour Breach Reporting SLA
raw_entities.description: Quantitative operational threshold mandating that all confirmed data security incidents must be formally reported to regulators and affected parties within exactly 72 hours of discovery.

properties.rule_statement: Comprehensive data protection framework governing privacy, subject rights, and breach reporting.

properties.name: Customer Communication and Fair Treatment Requirement

properties.rule_statement: Ensures all customer communications adhere to fair treatment standards.

properties.name: System Reliability and Business Continuity Requirement

properties.rule_statement: Maintains system reliability and ensures uninterrupted business operations during disruptions.
Enforces strict data security and privacy controls across all systems.
Monitors capital levels to ensure financial stability and regulatory compliance.
Ensures the organization can withstand, adapt to, and recover from operational disruptions.
Implements cyber security measures as a core component of operational resilience.
Guarantees data subject rights including access and deletion upon request.
Integrates data protection measures into the design and architecture of systems from inception.
Mandates timely notification of data breaches to relevant authorities.

properties.what_it_measures: Maximum allowable time to report a data breach after discovery
properties.context: Enforced under GDPR breach notification requirement

relationships.target_name: Customer Communication and Fair Treatment Requirement
relationships.description: FCA COBS mandates customer communication and fair treatment standards for banking operations
relationships.context:
**FCA COBS:**
- Customer communication and fair treatment

relationships.target_name: System Reliability and Business Continuity Requirement
relationships.description: FCA COBS mandates system reliability and business continuity standards to ensure service availability
relationships.context:
**FCA COBS:**
- System reliability and business continuity

relationships.description: FCA COBS mandates data security and privacy standards to protect customer information
PRA Requirements mandate capital adequacy monitoring to ensure financial stability and solvency
PRA Requirements mandate operational resilience standards to maintain critical services during disruptions
PRA Requirements mandate cyber security standards as a core component of operational resilience
GDPR defines the requirement for data subject rights including explicit access and deletion mechanisms
GDPR defines the requirement for data protection by design to embed privacy into system architecture
GDPR defines the requirement for breach notification procedures to ensure timely regulatory reporting
The breach notification requirement is quantified by a strict 72-hour reporting service level agreement
